Signatur e Table of Contents Introduction 4 Literature review. 6 Peculiarities of the in-game items market. 14 Selection of in-game items for investment. 19 Cases. 19 Skins. 25 Agents. 26 Sticker Capsules. 29 Creating an investment portfolio. 31 Comparison with other types of assets. 33 Comparison with other asset types separately 33 Adding in-game items to investment portfolio. 41 Conclusion 43 References 44 Appendix 48 Introduction In modern highly turbulent world, companies are looking for different ways to secure their assets. Some companies prefer to invest all available funds in the further development of the enterprise, while others create businesses in new industries that are not related to the industry in which the main business operates. A third way to secure assets is to invest  it into different types of assets . Currently, there are many different markets for investing with different level of risk. Federal bonds, shares of local or foreign companies, real estate are the main types of markets that companies choose as an investment opportunity. But in the twenty-first century, new markets have emerged that are entirely electronic - cryptocurrency markets and in-game items. And while the cryptocurrency market  is an established and relatively stable market that has been studied and described in a large number of scientific papers and articles, the market  of  in-game items is a market that is only in the stage of development and active growth . There are no publicly available works evaluating and analyzing this market. The goal of this paper is to assess the risks  and profits  of investing in the in-game item s  market and  to propose an investment portfolio of in-game items .  Subject:  Investment risks and Investment attractiveness of in-game items . Object: Steam In-game Items . Objectives: - Analysis of the specifics of the in-game items market . - Analysis of each group of in-game items . -  Creating  an investment portfolio . - Comparison of this portfolio with other financial assets  in term of yield/risk rate . - Comparison of the investment portfolio characteristics with and without in-game items included Relevance of the topic. The twenty-first century is the heyday of the digital world, which stimulates the emergence of new markets, such as the market  of  in-game items.  Technologies are developing with exponential acceleration, so it is  crucial  to be aware of modern technologies and markets,  since in the near future (for example, with the high integration of augmented reality into  our  li ves ) the digital resources market can expand in volumes and those who will understand it before others will be able to  enrich on it . It is also worth noting that in the current financial situation (due to covid-19 and geopolitical conflicts), most markets (stock market, cryptocurrency) have collapsed  (exact values will be provided in chapter “Comparison with other assets” ) , and only the in-game items market continues to grow at a high rate.  This fact indicates that the prices of in-game items do not depend on the financial situation in the world, but on other factors. This means that investing in in-game items will help diversify the investor's overall risks. The factors affecting the market of in-game items will be analyzed in this paper . From all of the above, it can be concluded that the chosen topic is particularly relevant at the present time. The following methods of scientific research were used in this paper:  m easurement, experiment, comparison. The next chapter will review the literature needed to familiarize with the methods of investing, diversification and risk assessment. Literature review. In this paper, in-game items are considered from the point of view of diversification of existing investment portfolios owned by private investors or corporations. Research shows that more than a quarter of all investors have only one share in their portfolio, more than half of investors have no more than three shares and more than 70 percent of investors have no more than five shares, and on average an investor has four shares in an investment portfolio (Equity Portfolio Diversification; William N.  Goetzmann , Alok Kumar . ) . These statistics clearly demonstrate that in modern society, investors prefer to invest their money in a small number of assets. But is this a winning strategy? As a result of the analysis of more than sixty thousand investment portfolios (Equity Portfolio Diversification; William N.  Goetzmann , Alok Kumar), it was found that the Sharpe coefficient corresponds to a lower level of well-being of investors whose portfolios are insufficiently diversified. This means that a small number of stocks in investment portfolios leads to lower results than portfolios with a variety of stocks. To confirm this fact, William N.  Getzmann  and Alok Kumar calculated that the least diversified portfolios, on average, generate 2.4% less profit per year than qualitatively diversified portfolios. This figure seems huge in the context of the fact that the largest American index funds (Dow Jones and S&P 500) have an average annual return of 11.6%. It is important to note that the investment portfolios of average investors who are insufficiently diversified exceed the indices of the stock market only in 9.53% of cases. That is, more than 90% of investors earned less than they could have if they had not taken into account their preferences, but invested in the market as a whole. The graph with the results of the analysis is given below. Figure  1 . Statistics of return rate of investors’ portfolios. Source - (https://academic.oup.com/rof/article/12/3/433/1598033) Moving from the general to the particular, it is important to note that the addition of in-game items to the investment portfolio should statistically increase the expected profitability of the portfolio, and since this market has a very weak connection with the stock market  and other established markets , the risks associated with global crises are reduced.  This  information is not enough to make a conclusion about whether it is worth investing in in-game items, but this information is enough to unequivocally state that the purpose of this article - the assessment of a new market as a possible asset for the diversification of investment portfolios - is relevant and useful for investors. In order to assess whether the investment portfolio has become more effective, it is necessary to apply one of the generally accepted measures of measuring efficiency. There are a large number of different coefficients that allow to assess the ratio of risk and profitability of investment portfolios or individual assets. In order to assess which of the methods of calculating risk and profitability is the most appropriate for this case, a brief comparative analysis of these coefficients was carried out. Coefficient of Variation. This ratio is used to compare assets with different levels of annual returns and standard deviations. It can be used to rank assets according to their degree of risk ( Coefficient of Variation ; Herve Abdi ). The higher the coefficient, the higher the risk of investing in this security. Since this coefficient mainly carries information about risks, ignoring the profitability of the stock, its use will not  provide  all the necessary information about investing in in-game items. Sharpe ratio. It is important to note that the Sharpe ratio is the most well-known and widely used coefficient for calculating investment risk, after the standard deviation and the coefficient of variation.  This coefficient was developed by William Sharpe, the Nobel Prize winner in economics, thereby complementing Markowitz's portfolio theory.  This coefficient corresponds to the return on each unit of risk. The main difference of this coefficient from all the others is that it is calculated based on the same principles used in Markowitz theory, i.e. instead of expectation of return the difference between the portfolio return  and the risk-free benchmark is calculated (Article: The Sharpe Ratio; William F. Sharpe in the book “Streetwise, The best of The Journal of Portfolio Management”; Peter L. Bernstein and Frank  J.Fabozzi  (169-175) ).   This ratio is calculated mainly for speculative, high-yield and high-risk assets.  Since the  goal  of this  paper  is precisely to find new assets for investment, it is important to understand the comparative performance relative to a risk-free investment ( eg , 30-year U.S. federal loan bonds), so that this coefficient will be more informative than the coefficient of variation. The information Ratio.  This ratio can be defined  as the additional amount of profit received per unit of additional risk. Like the Sharpe Ratio, this  r atio is considered relative to a benchmark (often a zero result or a federal loan bond). Varying from "-1" to "1" this coefficient can mean from "the analyst's forecasts have no connection with the market results" to "the market results are fully explained by the analyst's predictions". This coefficient is useful for measuring the overall performance of hedge funds and other organizations or managers, not individual stocks (The information Ratio; Thomas H.  Goodwin ( ) ) , so it will not be used for this paper. Sortino coefficient . The Sharpe and Sortino ratios are very similar, except that when calculating risk using the Sortino ratio, only returns below a certain level (basically, the return on a risk-free asset) are taken into account, and positive deviation returns are treated as risk-free because they strongly increase the return on the asset (Comparative Analysis of Sharpe and Sortino Ratio with reference to Top Ten Banking and Finance Sector Mutual Funds;  Paoja  Srivastava and Dr. Syed Shahid Mazhar. ). That is, when calculating the standard deviation, only the returns that will be lower than the returns of risk-free assets for the same period will be  taken into account . This  peculiarity  is very important in the context of the in-game items market, because due to the active growth of the game industry and the economy of in-game items in general, the market  shows active growth, which ,  when using the Sharpe ratio ,  will be interpreted as an incredibly high risk, although it is not. Therefore, it can be argued that in this situation the Sortino coefficient is more appropriate to apply than the Sharpe coefficient. But since the Sharpe ratio is a universally recognized coefficient, it will also be calculated and the results of the two calculations will be compared to get a broader picture. Treynor Ratio. The difference of the Traynor ration from the Sharpe Ratio again lies in the procedure of risk calculation: when calculating the Traynor Ratio, the beta coefficient is taken as the risk, reflecting the volatility of the asset in relation to the market (Investor-Specific Performance Measurement: A Justification of Sharpe Ratio and Treynor Ratio.; Hendrik Scholz, Marco Wilkens ). Thus, the Traynor coefficient shows the ratio of the additional return to the market risk. Since the in-game items market has no connection to the securities market and the cryptocurrency market, it makes no sense to use the relative volatility and, accordingly, the Traynor coefficient. Based on this analysis, it can be confidently stated that the Sortino coefficient is optimal for assessing the risk and return on assets in this Bachelor Thesis. The Sharpe coefficient will also be calculated in the work, as it is a generally recognized coefficient and will allow to evaluate assets in comparison with various  b enchmarks. Before  analyzing  the market of in-game items, it is important to assess the overall dynamics of the capitalization of this market and forecasts about  its  further development. The Covid-19 pandemic, which has changed the lives of most people in the world and turned the entire global economy upside down, has had a beneficial effect on the gaming industry. In the modern world, the online gaming industry has reached such a size that some corporations hold tournaments with prize funds up to 65,000,000 USD. This suggests that at the moment the gaming industry is a developed (or actively developing) market, in which relatively large amounts of money are involved (this money cannot be compared with the capitalization of  real  sports (for example, football), but the increase in this market segment  should be noticed . According to forecasts, the computer games market will grow from $229 billion in 2021 to $546 billion in 2028 (Gaming Market Size, Share & COVID-19 Impact Analysis; Fortune Business  Insights ). This trend is a positive factor affecting the market of in-game items. The more market participants  are in the market , the larger and more balanced this market will be. In order to understand the main characteristics of the market, it will be useful to understand the features of similar markets. For the market of in-game items, the most similar and at the same time having a large capitalization is the cryptocurrency market. Both markets are associated with trading virtual assets that have no connection with the real value (some cryptocurrencies are still backed by real assets or depend on their value, but in general there is no such dependence). Yanuar   Andrianto  and Yoda  Diputra  in the paper “The Effect of Cryptocurrency on Investment Portfolio Effectiveness” ( ) have identified the following features of the cryptocurrency market: - The standard deviation coefficient on the cryptocurrency market, depending on the chosen period, can reach values exceeding 100%. At the same time, the returns of cryptocurrency portfolios are much higher than those of the underlying assets.  - Cryptocurrency market is very volatile, while highly profitable, that is, it is a standard 'risky' asset which has a low level of connection to the body of the investment portfolio, as belongs to a different market. If the in-game asset market would have the same properties, it would make it very attractive to potential investors. It is important to note that the Sharpe coefficient for an investment portfolio reaches a relative maximum with the share of cryptocurrencies in the investment portfolio from 5% to 20%  (“The impact of cryptocurrencies on the effectiveness of the investment portfolio”  Januar   Andrianto ,   Yoda  Diputra ).  Based on this statement, when comparing investment portfolios without in-game items and with in-game items, a portfolio with in-game items will contain 20%  of in-game items . The in-game item market is a typical example of an ‘alternative’ asset. That is, having a weak connection with assets that make up the bulk of the investment portfolio.  Since this work is aimed, among other things, at analyzing changes in the risk and profitability of the average investment portfolio when adding in-game items to it, it is important to understand how the average portfolio reacts to the addition of other “alternative assets”. Jens Carsten  Jackwerth  and Anna  Slavutskaya  in their article “The total benefit of alternative assets to pension fund portfolios” ( ) studied the impact of seven different “alternative” assets on portfolio profitability. The results of the study suggest that the response of portfolio profitability strongly depends on the type of asset that is an ‘alternative asset’, so the very fact of adding an ‘alternative asset’ will not always lead to increased profits. This fact clearly demonstrates the importance of in-depth analysis of the market and the asset that is considered as an investment. Although the addition of ‘alternative’ assets ambiguously affects the profitability of the portfolio,  its  impact on the value of risk for the investment portfolio is quite unambiguous. Authors of the article (Are there any other safe haven assets? Evidence for “exotic” and alternative assets;  Dimitrios   Dimitriou , Dimitris  Kenourgios , Theodore Simos ) have proved that the aforementioned assets significantly reduce portfolio losses during the financial crisis, as well as reduce portfolio profitability during a bullish trend. It is important to note that the reduction in profitability is much lower than the reduction in risk, which makes the asset types under consideration attractive for reducing the riskiness of investment portfolios (each asset has unique characteristics, but the overall dynamics is common). Assets  that affect one of the risk/return parameters may not affect the second parameter so much, thereby increasing the value of the portfolio. It is this characteristic of in-game items (if any) that will be used to increase the profitability of the investment portfolio with a moderate increase in risk. As mentioned at the beginning of this chapter, the in–game items market has one very important feature relative to other well-known markets, such as the stock exchange market, the foreign exchange market or the cryptocurrency market - low liquidity. Since the gaming asset market is a  relatively small market (exact figures will be presented later in the article), when calculating an investment strategy, it is necessary to take into account the risk of insufficient liquidity and its impact on the return on investment and the possible amount of working capital. Scientists claim that low-liquid assets are 7.5% more profitable than highly liquid assets (Liquidity Risk and Expected   Stock Returns;  Ľuboš   Pástor  and Robert F. Stambaugh ). It is important to understand that the volatility of such stocks is much higher, and accordingly the riskiness of investing also increases. These conclusions are very important in the context of this  analysis  and will be taken into account when forming an investment portfolio. Summarizing the literature review, it is important to highlight the following conclusions: - The level of diversification of portfolios directly affects the returns an investor receives. This means that the analysis that will be carried out in this paper will help investors learn a new type of assets, thereby giving them more complete information to make a decision about portfolio diversification, which is statistically likely to have a positive impact on the returns on their investments. - When comparing methods of profitability and risk assessment of different investment portfolios, the Sortino coefficient, which is optimal for this market, was selected and will be used in this paper. - Estimates of scientists say that in the nearest years the games market and, consequently, in-game items will continue to develop, which should positively influence the investors' profitability. - A large number of scientific papers and studies support the hypothesis that alternative assets (cryptocurrency, wine, raw materials), although not all, but allow to increase the efficiency of investment portfolios. - Low liquid assets are more profitable and riskier relative to liquid assets.  There are plenty of research papers about diversification, valuing different markets and different assets, but there are no papers that evaluate the in-game item market from an investment perspective and compare it to other asset types. This analysis is the research gap that this paper  will address. By analyzing the existing market and its characteristics and compiling an investment portfolio, this paper will compare the risk-return ratio for (a) different asset types, including the compiled investment portfolio and (b) two investment portfolios, one of which will contain in-game items and one of which will not. Based on this analysis, it will be concluded that it is appropriate to consider in-game items as an asset for investment. In the next chapter the market of in-game items will be analyzed in order to evaluate the investment attractiveness of these items and make an investment portfolio . Peculiarities of the in-game items market.   The main feature of the market  of  in–game cosmetic items is that all assets traded  on  this market actually belong to the company that owns the game. Accordingly, there are many markets for cosmetic in-game items. In this paper, the market of the  “ Steam ”  trading platform will be studied.  Steam is an online digital distribution service for computer games . Over 44,000 game development companies have placed over 70,000 games on the Steam platform. This platform was chosen due to the fact that  it owns a share of more than 75% of the global PC games market, which makes this platform practically a monopolist of the market.  Items from 353 games are placed on the Steam trading platform  (as of  10 / 03 /2023) and each of these games has its own item s  market.   In this paper, the game Counter Strike: Global Offensive  ( CS:GO )  will be considered.  The developer of  CS:GO  is Valve,  which  also developed the Steam service itself.  This game is the most popular game on the Steam platform (a record number of simultaneous users - 1,519,457).  GS :GO  In-Game Item Market , a ccordin g  to the total capitalization of transactions,  occupies 97% of the entire market of the Steam trading platform.  CS:GO  is a multiplayer first–person shooter.  A terrorist team consisting of five players aims to kill all the players of the opposing team or detonate a bomb that needs to be  planted  in a certain place. Their opponent is a special forces team, also consisting of 5 players, aimed at neutralizing the opponent or neutralizing the bomb delivered. They fight using various types of weapons (61 types in total). Further in this chapter, the main features of the market of in-game cosmetic items of the game Counter Strike: Global Offensive on the Steam trading platform will be  discussed . 1. The  absence  of value outside the Steam trading platform.  In–game items of any game are the property of the developer company of this game and have no value outside the trading platform of this game (the service hosting this game). It is important to note that the Steam service allows  user  to replenish the user's internal wallet in various ways, but there is no withdrawal function. That is, as soon as a person has transferred funds to his gaming  account, he receives an equivalent amount of funds to his account, but this ‘money' no longer has its value outside the platform.  Due to the fact that many users of the Steam platform still want to exchange in-game currency for real money, while other users want to buy in-game currency cheaper than at the rate of 1:1,  a large number of services appeared on which players could buy in-game items from each other for real money.  One player transferred funds to the second, and the second sent the desired item to the first. These platforms have become very popular.  That is, in order to exchange the currency of the Steam service for real money,  user  have to use services to sell in-game items for real money. The absence of the value of items outside the Steam trading platform also makes it impossible to conduct any legally binding transactions, such as an act of sale. Theft of items also cannot be regarded as a crime.  The basic idea of all of the above is that all assets obtained as a result of investing in in-game items have zero real value until they are exchanged for real money.  It is important to note that with the profit received as a result of investment activities in the field of in-game items,  investor  still need to pay tax. In Russia, in the all-Russian classifier of economic activities, this activity will belong to category 62.09: Activities related to the use of computer technology and information technology, other. 2.  The dependence of the cost of items on the changing rules of the market. Game developers periodically make changes to the rules of market relations regarding the subjects of this game .  For example, in 2018, the developers of the game Player Unknown Battle Ground (PUBG) introduced a restriction on the exchange of in-game items, leaving the opportunity to sell and  buy   items on the market. Because of this, all the items from this game have dropped in price by an average of 75%.  This  case  is a clear example of the fact  that a game developer remains in full control of the market and this risk should be paid attention to when building an investment strategy. 3.  High commission. There is a very high transaction fee in all Steam in-game markets. In the case of  CS: GO  it is 13%.  That is, after selling an item worth 100 USD, the seller will receive only 87 USD. This is done so that the total amount of players' funds decreases, which would encourage them to invest additional funds.  It is important to note that we are also talking here not only about investors, but also about players. After all, the economy of this niche is designed specifically for players: the more players buy and sell, the less ‘money’ they have left, which forces them to replenish their wallets again. Investors, of course, take this commission into account when planning  their actions . 4.  Market size. According to estimates for 2022,  the volume of the Steam market is 3 billion USD. Since the  CS:GO   market  is the absolute monopolist of the Steam market (97%), then  the capitalization of all in-game cosmetic items of this game is approximately equal to 2.9 billion USD. For comparison, the securities market for 2019 amounted to 61 trillion USD , and the cryptocurrency market – 3.36 trillion . That is, the Steam market is currently a very small market.   It is also important  to take into account that each subject in  CS:GO  has its own “market” – the volume of purchase requests and lots for sale. At the moment (10.04.2023) on the  CS:GO  market there are 20,469  different items and the capitalization is divided between them. Some items are highly liquid , w hile other items are very low liquid. Low liquidity is the reason for several features of the  CS:GO  market: - High volatility. Due to the fact that the market for each item is small, the actions of one user or a group of people can significantly affect the price of the item. The higher the liquidity of the item, the more difficult it is to influence the price. - Limited working capital. A major player buying or selling a large volume of in-game items of the same type will influence the market  significantly . For example, picture #3 shows a list of lots for sale of the “Glove Case” position, after studying which it can be concluded that in order to raise the price of this item from 5.25 USD   per unit by 10% to 5.78 USD, it will be necessary to redeem 3028 cases at an average cost of 5.62 USD, investing 17,017 USD in total.  It is important to note that the increase in value will only be temporary, since a large number of items are held by investors and are not listed for sale. Figure  2 .  Buy orders of   “ Glove Case”  item. Of course, large investors collect their portfolio systematically so as not to influence the market, but nevertheless low liquidity is a strong limitation for large investors in Steam The mechanism of work of orders for the purchase of items. In the securities markets, the highest purchase request will be compared to the cheapest offer for sale. In the Steam market, the query system works differently. The cheapest offer for sale will be matched with a purchase request that - is not less than the amount of the offer for sale . -  Placed  before all other purchase requests satisfying the first rule . This mechanism of purchase requests allows  users  to buy significant amounts of items without affecting the market, although it will take some time. This feature of the market certainly needs to be used when buying items for investment, because in this way  investor  can save a significant amount of capital. In the following chapter, an analysis of each type of items will be carried out , according to the results of which it will either be added to the investment portfolio with the determination of the share in this portfolio, or it will not be. Selection of in-game items for investment. In this chapter, an analysis of each type of  items  in  CS :G O  will be carried out for investment attractiveness. At the end of the chapter, an investment portfolio of  CS:GO   in-game  items will be compiled. In Counter Strike: Global Offensive, there are several types of items and each of them  should  be considered separately in the prism of investment attractiveness . Types of items in  CS:GO : - Cases. Boxes that the player receives for free once a week after the game or by purchasing on the steam trading platform. By purchasing a key for 2.5 USD, the user can open the box and get a random “ s kin” – the appearance of in-game weapons . - Skins. The appearance of the game weapon. It has only a visual effect, does not give an advantage in the game.  User  can get it by opening a case or buying a certain skin on the steam trading platform. - Agents. The appearance of a game character. Visible only from the third -person view . It was possible to get it as part of special events, now only  by  buying on the steam trading platform. - Capsules with stickers. Analogous to cases, but  user  d oesn 't need a key to open the capsules. When opened, the user receives a random sticker that he can place on his weapon.  - Stickers on weapons. Stickers that can be placed on various positions of game weapons.  Players  can get it from capsules with stickers or on the steam trading platform. - Other in-game items. Also ,  there are such types of items as patches on agents, sets with music and collectible badges. Due to the fact that in total they occupy 2% of the market of  CS:GO  in-game  items and unlimited, they are not of interest as an object for investment and, accordingly, will not be considered in this paper. Cases . Once a week after the game, the user is given a random case for free. Inside this case there  is one out of  several external appearances of game weapons, the players call them “Skin". In order to try their luck and get a random “skin”, the user must buy a key  f or  this case for 2.5 USD. Figure  3 . Case opening page. At the moment (04.2023) there are 39 different cases. Five of them can be given to the user after the game with a chance of 19.8% each. In total, these five cases have a probability of 99%. The remaining cases (currently 34) fall out with equal probability, totaling the remaining 1% . These cases are called “from the rare list” .  As soon as the developer adds a new case to the game, this case also gets a 19.8% chance of falling out, respectively, one case is added to the rest and divides the remaining 1% chance of falling out.  When a case is added to the rare list, it becomes almost limited, but players continue to buy it on the  community market  and open it in order to get the desired skin. As the number of remaining cases decreases, and the demand remains constant, the price of the case on the trading  market  begins to grow. The  picture  below shows a graph of the cost of the first case in  CS:GO . At the time of its release, in 2014, it cost 0.04 USD. At the moment, this case can be purchased for 102 USD, which corresponds to an increase of 255,000% over nine years. Figure  4 . " CS:GO  Weapon Case" market page CS:GO  cases are the basis of the Steam economy, because  they are used by users to  get weapons – the main  items  on the market of this game. Considering  features of the cases, the following signs should be mentioned: - Practically limited . - Their number is constantly decreasing . - Their consumption is based on gambling, which suggests a constant interest and, accordingly, a decrease in quantity. - There is a stable system for adding cases to the rare list, which allows  investors  to predict the prices of certain cases. All of the above allows to say that investing in cases is an attractive investment. Later in this chapter, the methods of selecting cases  for investment  and their share in the portfolio will be described. In most investment portfolios in  CS:GO  cases make up the bulk of this portfolio. This is due to the fact that users open cases in huge quantities. And due to the fact that the cases from the rare list are almost limited, their cost begins to grow rapidly. Accordingly, it is most profitable to invest in cases that are opened most often. Since Valve does not advertise data on the number of  open cases, in order to determine which cases are opened most often, it is necessary to conduct a proper study. 1.1 Research: Which cases are opened most often? Since Valve (the developer of the game Counter Strike: Global Offensive) does not  share  this information, the number of open ed  cases of each type will be estimated by indirect signs. From each case,  player  can get weapons of different rarity. Some of these weapons are subsequently  listed  for sale on the steam  community market . The essence of the study is to compare the average number of lots for sale of items of the same rarity that can be obtained from different cases. To level out random deviations, the average number of lots for seven days (15.04.2023 – 21.04.2023)  will be compared .  All the calculations can be found via the link  to the google spreadsheet document (page “Case Popularity Coefficient”) . For each case, Case Popularity Coefficient will be calculated in the following way: average amount of items of the same rarity listed on the community market will be compared for each case. The lowe st  number will be corresponded for coefficient “1”. Other cases will have coefficient, equal for division of average amount of the items of the same rarity listed on the community market of this case by average amount of the items of the same rarity listed on the community market of the case with coefficient “1”. For a better understanding, calculations of this coefficient for the  item  “Revolution Case” will be given below. By opening this case, user can get 18  different  items of various rarity (there are five categories of rarity). To calculate this coefficient, only “restricted” rarity items will be analyzed.  In total, the Revolution Case can contain five items of this rarity: M4A1-S |  Emphorosaur -S, Glock-18 | Umbral Rabbit, MAC-10 |  Sakkaku , R8 Revolver | Banana Cannon, P90 |  Neoqueen . Then, during the week, the number of lots for the sale of each item on the  market  wa s recorded . At the end of the week, the average figures for the week were calculated. Next, the average index of  the ”restricted ” rarity items from the “Revolution Case” for the week was calculated, it is 192. This means that, on average, the item from this case was presented on the trading platform in the amount of 192.  Such calculations were made for all cases. The minimum value turned out to be 33, it was compared with a coefficient of 1. Accordingly, the “ R evolution Case” case has a popularity  coefficient  of 5.9. In the next part, skins  - the external appearance of game weapons  will be studied for investment .   Skins . The appearance of in-game items (players call them "skins") is a basic commodity on the steam trading  platform  and in the game  CS:GO . Skins do not affect the mechanics of the game, but only change the appearance of the game weapon. As mentioned earlier, there are 61 types of weapons in CS: GO. For each of these types of weapons, there are a large number of different  skins  that can be obtained from various cases.  As the number of rare-list cases on the trading  market  decreases over time , the number of skins from those cases also decreases, resulting in an increase in their cost. The following  Figure  shows a graph of the cost of one of the skins from the first case added to  CS:GO . In 2014, this weapon could be purchased for $9 USD, but in 2023 the price rose to $900 USD, showing a 10,000% increase over nine years. Figure  5 . "AWP Lightning Strike" market page Since skins can only be obtained from certain cases, the prices of skins depend on the prices of cases. The more expensive the case, the more expensive the attempt to get a particular skin, so fewer people open these cases, which also increases the price of skins. That is, both cases and skins have a basis for constant growth in price. In order to determine which asset type is more profitable to invest in, it is needed to determine which asset type shows more growth. This will mean that one of the asset types is more sensitive to changes in supply (per unit change in supply the price will change differently), and since supply for these asset types is related, the asset with the higher growth rate will be a more attractive asset to invest in. In order to estimate the difference in the growth rates of the value, it is necessary to estimate the average change in the prices of the cases and skins over the sample period (01.05.2022 - 01.04.2023) .  The calculations show that cases grew by an average of 217% over the period, while skins showed an increase of only 60%.  (All the calculations can be found in the “appendix” chapter, tables 10 and 11) From all of the above,  we can conclude that skins are an excellent "stabilizer" of the value of the financial portfolio, as prices for them change much more slowly than for cases. Since this financial portfolio is designed for risky investments, and therefore included in the investor's 20% of high-yield but high-risk assets, skins will not be included in this portfolio. The next chapter will analyze the investment attractiveness of agents, the external appearance of the game character. Agents. Agents in the game Counter Strike: Global Offensive - external appearance of the game character, which is visible only from the third person, that is to other players, not the owner of the agent. An important distinguishing feature of the agents is that  user  can get them only during special events - in the game they are called operations. Operations are thematic events in the game, which include a new game mode and a large number of tasks, for the completion of which the player receives points that can be spent on thematic items: stickers, weapons and agents. Agents have been available for purchase for the last three operations ( Shattered Web , Broken Fang, and  Riptide ). It is important to note that agent types are unique. That is, at the end of the operation, all agents of a given operation become limited. Since a large number of players buy agents on the Steam trading  market  after the end of the operation, the cost of these agents gradually increases . It is important to note that there is no guarantee that when the next operation is added (on average, operations are added annually), new agents will be added . For example, the first agents that were added to the game as part of Operation  Shattered Web  in November 2019 increased in price by an average of 400% over 3.5 years. These numbers are pretty impressive.  To answer the question about the investment appeal of agents, the following questions should be answered : - What has been the average increase in value shown by all agents over the past year? - How does the price for agents of old operations change when a new operation with agents  is added ? If the increase in cost over the last year is impressive (more than 50%), it would mean that users are still strongly interested in buying agents. And since the number of agents is not increasing, the cost continues to grow. It is important to analyze the change in the cost of agents of old operations when a new operation with agents  is added . If the old agents do not drop in value much (up to 20%), then if  its  comply with the first point, this investment can be considered attractive. It is important to remember that there is no guarantee that agents will be added when the next operation is added. In this case  investors  can expect a rapid growth of agents from the old collection s . Calculation of the average growth of the value of agents for the last year. (01.05.2022 - 01.05.2023) In order to estimate the average increase in the cost of agents for the period, the coefficients of the difference of cost (cost 01.05.2023 / cost 01.05.2022) for the agents of each operation  were calculated , after which their average value  was calculated .  (All the calculations can be found in the “Appendix” chapter, table 12.) The calculations showed that during this period the agents increased in price by an average of 188%. Such results indicate a very high demand for this type of in-game items and insufficient (and limited) supply. Certainly, this type of item should be considered  as  an investment asset. But in order to argue that,  it is  need ed  to estimate the effect of new agents'  emergence  at the time of adding a new operation on the price of existing agents. There is a high probability (it is impossible to estimate it mathematically, because the decision to add an operation is made by the game developers and there is no exact timetable for the release of such operations. The community has estimated that an operation is usually added once a year, and at this point the operation has not been added for more than 15 months. This is an indirect sign that the operation may be added soon, which means that the probability of new  agents’ emergence  increases) . To assess the impact of the release of new agents on the price of existing ones, analyze  of  the cost of the agents of Operation Broken Fang (added to the game on 04.12.2020) before and after the release of agents of Operation  Riptide  (added to the game on 22.09.2021)  will be conducted. Before the release of the agents of operation " Riptide ", the agents of operation "Broken Fang" cost on average (as of 01.09.2021) 4.26 USD, and after (as of 01.10.2021) - 3.08 USD (All calculations are presented in the  “Appendix” chapter, table 13 ) . Accordingly, the release of the new operation entailed a decline in the value of existing agents by an average of 29%. This fall is quite significant and its probability must be taken into account when compiling an investment portfolio. Nevertheless, the annual return is more than six times higher than the potential drop, so agents remain a very attractive investment object and will be added to the investment portfolio . The next chapter will analyze the last type of items in the game, sticker capsules, for their investment appeal . Sticker Capsules. Sticker capsules are one of the most popular items in  Counter- Strike:Global  Offensive.  Every year the company-developer of the game  CS:GO  holds championships, to which they invite the best eSports teams in the world in order to determine the best roster. These tournaments are analogous to world championships in other sports. Depending on their level of play and recent achievements, teams can qualify for three different tournament  stages : - Challengers (first stage of the tournament, 16 teams compete for 8 spots in the Contenders stage, where there are already 8 teams with a direct invite) - Contenders (second  stage  of the tournament, 16 teams compete for 8 spots in the Legends stage, where there are already 8 teams with direct invitations) - Legends. Third stage of the tournament, 16 teams compete to win the tournament . Before the beginning of the tournament, when the participants of each stage are already determined, the developer adds to the game three capsules with stickers with the logos of teams participating in each stage of the championship. During the championship these capsules can be purchased in the in-game store for 1 USD, after the championship the developer introduces a 75% discount, reducing the cost of capsules to 0,25 USD. A month after the end of the tournament (approximately, the exact numbers are always different) capsules are removed from the in-game store, thus becoming limited. To answer the question about the investment appeal of sticker capsules in the  CS:GO  game, same questions as when evaluating agents  should be answered , namely : - What has been the average increase in cost for all sticker capsules over the past year? - How does the price of old tournament sticker capsules change when a new tournament starts ? In order to estimate the average increase of sticker capsule prices for the previous year (01.05.2022 - 01.05.2023), the average percentage price change of sticker capsules from the last  five tournaments that ended before 01.05.2022 (Stockholm 2021, 2020 RMR, Berlin 2019, Katowice 2019 and London 2018) for this interval  will be calculated .   (All the calculations can be found in the “Appendix” chapter, table #14) Calculations have shown that the average ratio of prices 01.05.2023 to prices 01.05.2022 is 2.57. That is, on average, capsules with stickers for this period rose by 157%. This suggests that players are strongly interested in buying and opening these capsules, and their limitation leads to higher prices. While this result is not as high as that of cases or agents, it is nevertheless outstanding. At this point in the analysis, we can assume that sticker capsules are an attractive investment. To be sure of this,  it is necessary  to assess the reaction of already released sticker capsules to the addition of new capsules to the game. If the drop in value is well below the annual average increase in value, this type of in-game item will be added to the investment portfolio. The reaction of 2020 RMR capsules to Stockholm 2021 tournament capsules and the reaction of Stockholm 2021 tournament capsules to Antwerp 2022 tournament capsules will be evaluated . The calculation results clearly show that at the time of the Stockholm 2021 capsule release (10/29/2021), the capsules of the previous 2020 RMR tournament fell in value by 20%  (All the calculations can be found in the “Appendix” chapter, table #15) , while at the time of the Antwerp 2022 capsule release (05/11/2022), the capsules of the previous Stockholm 2021 tournament fell by 25%.  (All the calculations can be found in the “Appendix” chapter, table #16) Again, this drop may be perceived as strong for the stock market, but in the context of in-game items, where capsules have an average annual growth rate of over 150%, this risk does not greatly affect the outcome. In addition, it is important to note that at the moment (01.05.2023) the Paris 2023 tournament capsules are expected to be released in the coming days, which is likely to affect the value of capsules from previous tournaments, thereby creating a favorable entry point into these investments. To sum it up, it is safe to say that  CS:GO  game sticker capsules are a great investment opportunity that will be added to investment portfolio. To do so,  it is needed to  determine the sum of the squares of the "Loss relative to the risk-free asset (%)" values for each asset, divide this sum by the number of values analyzed (in  studied  case - 11) and take the square root of the resulting value. The Sharpe Ratio will also be calculated for these types of assets and then a conclusion will be made about the comparative characteristics of these assets based on both ratios. This coefficient was developed by William Sharpe in 1966 during the development of H. Markowitz's portfolio theory  as a tool for assessing the profitability to risk ratio of an investment portfolio. To calculate this ratio, it is necessary to divide the return on a risk-free asset (the mathematical expectation of the return based on historical information) by the risk (the standard deviation of the asset prices in the studied period). The final value reflects the number of units of return obtained per unit of risk above the return on the risk-free asset. This coefficient differs from the Sortino coefficient in that it also takes into account the positive dynamics of the portfolio value as a risk. This should have a strong effect on the coefficient value for the in-game item portfolio, as a growth of about 200% over the year would be interpreted as high risk. The next chapter will summarize the results of this work . Conclusion The aim of this work was to assess the  methods, risks and  feasibility of investing in the in-game items market. In the chapter "Literature review" the methods of evaluation of profitability and riskiness of investment assets were analyzed and the appropriate one was chosen. The next chapter described the main features of the in-game item market. After that, each type of in-game items was analyzed separately, on the basis of which an investment portfolio was compiled, which was compared with other asset types in the next chapter. As a result of all of the above, the following conclusions can be drawn: - Based on the analysis of in-game items, a list of methods (types of items) attractive for investment was determined - Based on an analysis of the in-game item market, an investment portfolio was compiled. - The coefficient reflecting the return per unit risk of the asset showed that in-game items are currently an excellent investment asset, leading over securities of large U.S. companies, the S$P 500 index and the  “ 10 Crypto ind ex ” . - At the same time, the in-game asset market has a number of limitations, including low market size, private corporation controllability and high volatility . To summarize, it is safe to say that in-game items, though a new and unfamiliar asset, can already be considered as a full-fledged investment object with very high return characteristics and low risk.  On the other side, liquidity of this market is comparatively low  nowadays , which can  make difficulties for “big” investors.   Nevertheless, in-game items should still be considered as a high-risk asset and not more than 20% of investment capital should be allocated to it according to the Pareto rule .
